title: Revolt of 1857
content: The Revolt of 1857 was the first major uprising against British rule in India. It began as a sepoy mutiny in Meerut and spread across North India. Leaders like Rani Lakshmibai, Bahadur Shah Zafar, and Nana Sahib played key roles. The revolt exposed weaknesses in British control and led to the end of East India Company rule in India.
hindi: 1857 का विद्रोह भारत में ब्रिटिश शासन के खिलाफ पहला बड़ा आंदोलन था। यह मेरठ में सिपाही विद्रोह के रूप में शुरू हुआ और पूरे उत्तर भारत में फैल गया।

title: Non-Cooperation Movement
content: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 to oppose British rule peacefully. Indians were urged to boycott foreign goods, schools, and titles. The movement increased mass participation in the freedom struggle. Though it ended in 1922 after the Chauri Chaura incident, it marked a turning point in India’s national movement.
hindi: असहयोग आंदोलन 1920 में महात्मा गांधी द्वारा शुरू किया गया था। इसमें ब्रिटिश वस्तुओं और संस्थानों का बहिष्कार किया गया।

title: Quit India Movement
content: The Quit India Movement was launched in 1942 demanding an immediate end to British rule. It led to mass protests, strikes, and arrests across India. Though leaders were jailed, the movement showed strong determination for independence and weakened British authority in the final phase of the freedom struggle in India.
hindi: भारत छोड़ो आंदोलन 1942 में शुरू हुआ था जिसका उद्देश्य ब्रिटिश शासन को तुरंत समाप्त करना था।

title: Civil Disobedience Movement
content: The Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930 with Mahatma Gandhi’s Dandi March against the salt tax. It encouraged Indians to break unjust laws peacefully and refuse cooperation with British authorities. The movement spread nationwide and strengthened the demand for independence. It played a key role in mobilizing people and challenging colonial policies effectively.
hindi: सविनय अवज्ञा आंदोलन 1930 में दांडी मार्च से शुरू हुआ। इसमें अन्यायपूर्ण कानूनों का विरोध किया गया।

title: Jallianwala Bagh Massacre
content: The Jallianwala Bagh Massacre occurred in 1919 when General Dyer ordered troops to fire on a peaceful gathering in Amritsar. Hundreds of unarmed people were killed, causing widespread anger across India. The incident exposed the brutality of British rule and became a turning point, strengthening nationalist sentiments and accelerating the struggle for independence.
hindi: 1919 में जलियांवाला बाग में जनरल डायर ने शांतिपूर्ण सभा पर गोलीबारी करवाई, जिससे कई लोग मारे गए।

title: Regulating Act 1773
content: The Regulating Act of 1773 was the first step taken by the British Parliament to control the East India Company’s administration in India. It created the post of Governor-General of Bengal and introduced a system of governance. This act marked the beginning of British governmental control and laid the foundation for future administrative reforms.
hindi: 1773 का रेगुलेटिंग एक्ट ब्रिटिश संसद द्वारा ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी के शासन को नियंत्रित करने का पहला प्रयास था।

title: Pitt’s India Act 1784
content: Pitt’s India Act of 1784 established dual control over the East India Company by introducing the Board of Control. It allowed the British government to supervise political affairs in India while the company handled commercial matters. This act strengthened British authority and improved administrative efficiency, making it important in constitutional history.
hindi: 1784 का पिट्स इंडिया एक्ट ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी पर ब्रिटिश सरकार का नियंत्रण बढ़ाने के लिए बनाया गया था।

title: Indian National Congress 1885
content: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885 and became a major platform for political dialogue and reform. Initially moderate in approach, it later led the struggle for independence. Leaders like Dadabhai Naoroji and Gokhale guided its early phase. The Congress played a central role in uniting Indians against colonial rule.
hindi: भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस की स्थापना 1885 में हुई और यह स्वतंत्रता संग्राम का प्रमुख संगठन बना।

title: Swadeshi Movement 1905
content: The Swadeshi Movement started in 1905 after the partition of Bengal. It encouraged Indians to use local goods and boycott foreign products. The movement promoted national pride and self-reliance. It also led to the growth of indigenous industries and education institutions, making it an important phase in the early nationalist movement.
hindi: स्वदेशी आंदोलन 1905 में बंगाल विभाजन के विरोध में शुरू हुआ और स्वदेशी वस्तुओं के उपयोग को बढ़ावा दिया गया।

title: Simon Commission 1927
content: The Simon Commission was appointed in 1927 to review constitutional reforms in India. It had no Indian members, which led to widespread protests across the country. Indians opposed it strongly with the slogan Simon Go Back. The protests highlighted the demand for self-governance and unity among Indians during the freedom struggle.
hindi: साइमन कमीशन 1927 में बिना किसी भारतीय सदस्य के बनाया गया, जिससे पूरे देश में विरोध हुआ।
